summary: Typosquat dropper impersonating Anthropic SDK fetches and executes remote Python payload at install time
details: free-anthropic-claude impersonates the official Anthropic Claude SDK but contains no Anthropic API code. package.json declares `postinstall: node lib/cli.js`, which on `npm install` reaches code in lib/index.js that constructs sensitive APIs via string-array obfuscation (e.g. `['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['htt','ps'].join('')`, `['spa','wn'].join('')`, `['write','File','Sync'].join('')`, `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `module['constr'+'uctor']['_l'+'oad']`) to evade static scanners. The postinstall code POSTs an auth token over TLS (with certificate validation disabled) to one of four hardcoded bare IPs â€” 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146 on port 8443 â€” receives base64-encoded files (including main.py), writes them to a masquerading directory chosen per platform (`~/.local/share/com.apple.sync` on macOS, `~/.local/share/prometheus` on Linux, `%APPDATA%\Local\Microsoft\Windows Security\Health` on Windows), force-installs Python via winget/brew/miniconda along with `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `psutil`, and `eth-account` (the canonical crypto-wallet/seed-phrase stealer toolkit), and spawns the payload detached with `_INTERNAL=1`. A `.cs_v2` marker enables persistence â€” subsequent loads respawn the dropped Python without re-fetching. On macOS, the code additionally tampers with the user's TCC privacy database via sqlite3 `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO access` to silently grant `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles`/`SystemPolicyAppData` to Terminal, node, and /usr/bin/python3, bypassing macOS privacy prompts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
